# Copilot PR Review Instructions — Survey Solutions

Project-specific pitfalls to flag during PR review. Use **in addition to** `.github/copilot-instructions.md` (which defines severity format, scope, and general review rules).

> **Scope reminder:** review only lines added/modified in the diff. Apply the same severity prefixes (`🔴 CRITICAL`, `🟠 HIGH`, `🟡 MEDIUM`) and output format defined in `copilot-instructions.md`. Skip findings below 🟡 MEDIUM. Style/formatting is out of scope.

---

## 1. 🔴 Edits to generated `.cshtml` files (Vite build output)

Some `.cshtml` files are **regenerated** by the Vite build in `src/UI/WB.UI.Frontend/vite.config.js` from sibling `*.Template.cshtml` sources. Manual edits to the generated file are silently overwritten on next build.

**Generated (do not edit directly):**

| Destination project | Generated file | Edit this instead |
|---|---|---|
| `WB.UI.Headquarters.Core` | `Views/Shared/_Layout.cshtml` | `_Layout.Template.cshtml` |
| `WB.UI.Headquarters.Core` | `Views/Shared/_EmptyLayout.cshtml` | `_EmptyLayout.Template.cshtml` |
| `WB.UI.Headquarters.Core` | `Views/Shared/_Logon.cshtml` | `_Logon.Template.cshtml` |
| `WB.UI.Headquarters.Core` | `Views/Shared/_FinishInstallation.cshtml` | `_FinishInstallation.Template.cshtml` |
| `WB.UI.Headquarters.Core` | `Views/WebInterview/_WebInterviewLayout.cshtml` | `_WebInterviewLayout.Template.cshtml` |
| `WB.UI.Headquarters.Core` | `Views/WebInterview/Index.cshtml` | `Index.Template.cshtml` |
| `WB.UI.Headquarters.Core` | `Views/UnderConstruction/Index.cshtml` | `Index.Template.cshtml` |
| `WB.UI.WebTester` | `Views/Shared/_Layout.cshtml` | `_Layout.Template.cshtml` |

**Flag:** diff modifies any file in the left column. If the change is a committed re-build (asset-hash filenames like `main.<hash>.js|css` updated inside the template), it is intentional and should **not** be flagged — see false-positive rule in `copilot-instructions.md`. Flag only hand-written logic/markup edits.

```
🟠 HIGH: Edit to generated Razor layout
File: src/UI/WB.UI.Headquarters.Core/Views/Shared/_Layout.cshtml
Problem: This file is regenerated by Vite from _Layout.Template.cshtml on next build; the change will be overwritten.
Fix: Move the edit into _Layout.Template.cshtml.
```

---

## 2. 🟠 Edits to committed JS / CSS build output

Compiled frontend assets are committed but must never be hand-edited.

**Build output roots:**
- `src/UI/WB.UI.Designer/wwwroot/js/`, `wwwroot/css/` (Vite → Designer static files)
- Any `dist/` folder under `src/UI/WB.UI.Frontend/` or its output targets
- `.vite/` manifest folders

**Flag:** hand-authored changes to `.js`, `.mjs`, `.css`, `.map` files under those paths. The fix is to edit the source under `src/UI/WB.UI.Designer/questionnaire/` or `src/UI/WB.UI.Frontend/src/` and rebuild.

Asset-hash filename churn (e.g., `app.<hash>.js` rename) produced by a legitimate rebuild is acceptable — do not flag.

---

## 3. Do your best

---

## 4. 🔴 `@Html.Raw(...)` on untrusted data

`@Html.Raw` disables Razor's HTML encoding. It is only safe for:
- Hard-coded resource strings from `*.resx` (e.g., `QuestionnaireController.Delete`) that contain formatting tags.
- Server-generated values explicitly sanitized via `StringExtensions.RemoveHtmlTags()` (Ganss.Xss).
- `JsonConvert.SerializeObject(...)` output emitted inside a `<script>` block **when** the value is not user-controlled.

**Flag** any `@Html.Raw` on:
- `Model.*`, `ViewData[...]`, `TempData[...]`, route data, or anything derived from an HTTP request.
- Concatenated strings that mix a resource template with a user-supplied value (e.g., `string.Format(res, Model.Name)` inside `Html.Raw` — the `{0}` slot will receive unescaped user input).

Default-safe replacements: remove `Html.Raw` (let Razor encode), or sanitize with `RemoveHtmlTags()` first, or emit as JSON via `@Json.Serialize(...)` when the consumer is JavaScript.

```
🔴 CRITICAL: Stored XSS via Html.Raw on user data
File: src/UI/WB.UI.Designer/Views/Questionnaire/Foo.cshtml, line N
Problem: @Html.Raw(Model.Comment) renders a user-supplied string as HTML without sanitization; an authenticated user can store <script> that executes in every reviewer's session.
Fix: Drop Html.Raw (so Razor encodes), or sanitize with .RemoveHtmlTags() before rendering.
```

---

ASP.NET Core antiforgery is enforced on state-changing endpoints. Browser-side mutating calls must send the token; the established helper is `getCsrfCookie()` (see `src/UI/WB.UI.Designer/Scripts/custom/common.js`).

Exception: calls to endpoints explicitly decorated `[IgnoreAntiforgeryToken]` (rare — check the controller). Even then, prefer adding the header for defense in depth.

```
🔴 CRITICAL: CSRF token missing on mutating AJAX call
File: src/UI/WB.UI.Designer/Scripts/custom/foo.js, line N
Problem: The new POST to /api/foo does not send X-CSRF-TOKEN; the request will be rejected by antiforgery in production, and if antiforgery is relaxed the endpoint is exposed to CSRF.
Fix: Add headers: { 'X-CSRF-TOKEN': getCsrfCookie() } the way the other mutating calls in common.js do.
```

---

## 6. 🟡 New Vue route missing guard / registration wiring

### HQ / WebInterview (`WB.UI.Frontend`)

Routes are contributed by **component providers** (classes consumed by `ComponentsProvider`, see `src/UI/WB.UI.Frontend/src/hqapp/ComponentsProvider.js`). The provider's `beforeEnter` is the hook where Vuex modules are registered and view `initialize()` runs. Defining a new route **outside** this pipeline skips module registration — the view will mount against a store that has no state for it.

**Flag** when the diff:
- Adds a new entry to `routes` array in `hqapp/router/index.js` or `webinterview/router/index.js` **without** going through a provider class that declares `modules` / `initialize`, or
- Adds a new component provider but forgets to register it in the provider list passed to `ComponentsProvider`.

Also flag new authenticated routes that skip the global `router.beforeEach` (e.g., by mounting their own router instance).

### Designer (`WB.UI.Designer/questionnaire`)

New routes must be registered centrally in `questionnaire/src/router/index.js`. Flag route additions made by ad-hoc `router.addRoute(...)` calls scattered in components.

### i18n for new pages (both frontends)

User-visible strings introduced by a new page must use the existing i18next instance (`$t(...)` / `i18n.t(...)`). Flag hardcoded English text in new `.vue` templates or new JS strings shown to the user. This rule is already in `copilot-instructions.md` §"Vue / Frontend" — report once per PR, not per occurrence.

```
🟡 MEDIUM: New HQ route bypasses ComponentsProvider
File: src/UI/WB.UI.Frontend/src/hqapp/router/index.js, line N
Problem: The new /reports/custom route is pushed directly into the routes array instead of being contributed by a component provider; the Vuex module it depends on is never registered, causing undefined-store errors on first navigation.
Fix: Expose the route from a provider class (with `modules` and optional `initialize`) and add that class to the provider list consumed by ComponentsProvider.
```

---

## Quick Checklist

When reviewing a PR, scan the diff specifically for:

- [ ] Edits to generated `.cshtml` (section 1) — direct content change beyond Vite asset-hash churn.
- [ ] Edits to files under `wwwroot/js`, `wwwroot/css`, `dist/`, `.vite/` (section 2).
- [ ] New `@Html.Raw(...)` — argument provably non-user-controlled or sanitized? (section 4)
- [ ] New mutating AJAX call — `X-CSRF-TOKEN` header present? (section 5)
- [ ] New Vue route — registered through `ComponentsProvider` (HQ) or central router (Designer), and visible strings localized? (section 6)

If none trigger and no higher-severity issue is present, respond: `✅ No issues found in the diff.`
